cases.name: allowlisted_200
steps: set the source origin to the allowlisted origin
send the request to the protected endpoint
assert the response status is 2xx
assert the protected resource data is present

cases.name: non_allowlisted_403
steps: set the source origin to a non-allowlisted origin
send the request to the protected endpoint
assert the response status is the documented block status
assert no_data: no protected resource data is returned

cases.name: xff_spoof_403
steps: set the true peer origin to a non-allowlisted origin
add a forwarded-for header naming an allowlisted origin
send the request to the protected endpoint
assert the client-supplied forwarded header is ignored and the true peer origin governs
assert the block status stands
assert no_data: no protected resource data leaks

cases.name: cidr_subnet
steps: pick an origin inside the documented allowed CIDR range
pick a sibling origin one address beyond the mask boundary
send both requests to the protected endpoint
assert the in-range origin is allowed 2xx with data present
assert the sibling-outside origin is blocked with no_data returned

cases.name: ipv6
steps: set an IPv6 source origin matching the brief's allow or deny expectation
send the request to the protected endpoint
assert the outcome follows the IPv6 origin's allowlist membership
assert data is present only when the origin is allowed

cases.name: multi_hop_xff_depth
steps: build a forwarded-for chain longer than the configured trusted-proxy depth
send the request through the trusted proxy to the protected endpoint
assert the client origin chosen is the one at the configured trusted-proxy depth
assert untrusted hops are ignored
assert allow-or-deny follows that depth-selected origin's membership

cases.name: denylist_precedence
steps: ensure the origin appears on both the allowlist and the denylist
send the request to the protected endpoint
assert the denylist takes precedence
assert no_data: no protected resource data is returned

cases.name: allowlist_add_allows
steps: call the allowlist management endpoint to add a formerly-blocked origin
re-send the request from that origin to the protected endpoint
assert the origin is now allowed 2xx
assert the protected resource data is present

cases.name: allowlist_remove_blocks
steps: call the allowlist management endpoint to remove a formerly-allowed origin
re-send the request from that origin to the protected endpoint
assert the origin is now blocked
assert no_data: no protected resource data is returned
